step: Inspect the existing board-health decision path in `codex-dashboard/server.js` together with the shared metrics/task-record parsing in `scripts/lib.sh`, then identify the smallest persisted-record inputs already available for two deterministic signals: retry churn from active/recent multi-attempt retrying work and queue starvation from pending/approved backlog without active progress.

decision_path.healthy_flip_rule: A fresh strategy run is forced from `running` to `failed` only when `buildStrategyHealthGuard(...).healthy` is false.

why_needed: Scope retry and starvation checks to one project
Detect actionable active work from `status` and `execution.state`
Detect in-progress retry evidence from `execution.state`, `execution.attempt`, and `execution.max_retries`

fields: non-empty line count per project queue file
why_needed: Detect whether approved/pending backlog is actually queued
Provide recent completed multi-attempt outcomes with deterministic `attempts` data
Allow a bounded recent retry-churn signal without inventing new history storage

entrypoints: provider-stats recovery merge from registry + tasks.log
shared_task_record_parsing_in_scripts_li.note: The shared shell parsing already normalizes the same project/task keys used by the dashboard, and it also proves that retry attempts are persisted both in registry execution data and in `tasks.log` records.

retry_churn.rule: Use registry records for active churn and task-log records for recent churn. Active retry churn is already derivable when at least 1 project task is still actionable (`status in {"approved","running"}` OR `execution.state in {"running","retrying"}`) and shows retry evidence (`execution.state == "retrying"` OR (`execution.attempt > 1` AND (`execution.max_retries == 0` OR `execution.attempt <= execution.max_retries`))). Recent retry churn can be derived from project `tasks.log` records by selecting recent completed outcomes with `attempts > 1` and `result in {"SUCCESS","FAILURE"}`.
retry_churn.why_bounded: This stays within persisted per-task fields that are already loaded by `readStrategyHealth` and already written by `append_task_log_record`, so no new storage format or non-deterministic history scan is required.

queue_starvation.rule: Detect queue starvation for a project when backlog is present in persisted registry state (`pending_approval + approved > 0`), but there is no active progress (`execution.state in {"running","retrying"}` count is 0) and no queued work for the project (`queues/<project>.txt` line count is 0). Pending backlog is already counted by `buildProjectHealthMetrics`, approved backlog is already counted by the same path, and live activity is already derived by `compactQueueState`.
queue_starvation.why_bounded: This uses only existing persisted registry, queue, and runtime-status inputs, with one fixed backlog threshold of `>= 1` task across pending or approved work and no new timer-based heuristic.
